title: daemon/store: get_spaces(ids=None) has no caller-side limit — full member table scan possible
description: get_spaces(ids=None, account_id) (store.rs line 638) runs: SELECT s.id ... FROM space s WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM space_member sm WHERE sm.space_id = s.id AND sm.contact_id = ?) ORDER BY s.created_at ASC. There is no LIMIT clause. If an account_id is a member of a very large number of spaces (e.g., due to a bug that adds them to many spaces), this returns an unbounded result set. The individual Space/get handler passes None when ids is absent in the JMAP request. The same pattern exists in query_spaces (line 699). Unlike get_contacts and get_chats which have LIMIT 1000, get_spaces has no bound. Trigger: account member of many spaces + Space/get with no ids filter. Bad behavior: unbounded row fetch, large JSON response, potential OOM on relay.
